name: Chase
description: An AI pursuit behavior where an agent moves toward a target or last known position until it catches, loses, or abandons the target.
design_notes: Chase is a contract about pressure and escape. The behavior needs target_id, last_known_position, line_of_sight_grace_ms, path_replan_interval_ms, pursuit timeout, and clear transitions back to search or patrol so players understand when they have escaped.
design_purpose: Convert detection into immediate spatial pressure.
Create readable escape, kiting, or repositioning scenarios.
Connect perception, navigation, combat range, and alert systems into one high-priority AI state.
player_fantasy: The player feels hunted and rewarded for breaking sightlines, using terrain, or reaching safety at the right moment.

parameters.name: target_id
parameters.description: Current actor or stimulus source the AI is pursuing.

parameters.name: last_known_position
parameters.description: Most recent validated location used when direct perception is lost.

parameters.name: line_of_sight_grace_ms
parameters.description: Grace window after sight breaks before switching to search or giving up.

parameters.name: path_replan_interval_ms
parameters.description: Minimum interval between navigation path recalculations during pursuit.

parameters.name: pursuit_timeout_ms
parameters.description: Maximum time the AI remains in chase without reacquiring the target or reaching last_known_position.

edge_cases: The target teleports, climbs, enters a vehicle, or crosses an off-navmesh link the chaser cannot use.
A navmesh_query fails repeatedly and the AI must decide whether to wait, switch route, search, or abandon pursuit.
Target_id ownership changes in multiplayer and chase authority must remain server-side.
The target becomes hidden or invulnerable while still inside attack range.
common_bugs: Path replans every frame and tanks performance when many agents chase the same target.
last_known_position updates through walls because perception_event filtering does not require visibility or valid sound propagation.
Chase animation speed and navigation velocity desynchronize, causing sliding or unfair reach.
line_of_sight_grace_ms is missing hysteresis, so the AI flickers between chase and patrol at doorway edges.
balancing_notes: Short grace windows make stealth resets easy; long windows create pressure but need cover and escape routes.
Pursuit speed should be tuned against player stamina, traversal tools, and level geometry, not in isolation.
Groups of chasers need coordination or spawn budgets; many perfect pursuers can remove player agency.
accessibility_notes: Show chase state with redundant cues such as music, UI alert markers, enemy barks, or camera-safe indicators.
Avoid chase sequences that require perfect audio localization unless visual route and threat cues are also available.

implementation_notes.unity: Use NavMeshAgent path queries at path_replan_interval_ms, store target_id and last_known_position in AI state, and draw debug lines for current path, perception_event source, and give-up timer.
implementation_notes.godot: Use NavigationAgent2D/3D with a chase state that only refreshes paths on a timer; keep last_known_position and line_of_sight_grace_ms separate from animation playback.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use AI Perception plus Behavior Tree/StateTree Blackboard keys for TargetActor, LastKnownPosition, and PursuitTimeout; server authority should own target selection in networked play.
implementation_notes.web: Run chase decisions on fixed simulation ticks with deterministic target_id selection and throttled pathfinding; keep animation interpolation separate from authoritative movement state.

example_games.title: Pac-Man
example_games.note: Uses enemy pursuit as a core pressure mechanic.

example_games.title: Left 4 Dead
example_games.note: Uses pursuit and horde pressure around player position.
